criteria.evidence: Days until interview: **10** ... Rounds: **recruiter screen (30m) → technical screen (60m...) → onsite (coding + system design + behavioral)**
criteria.reason: Timeline and full round structure captured under Constraints and actively used to drive the day-by-day cadence.

criteria.evidence: Day 2/3 build-stories ... Day 5 SQL track (technical screen has live coding) ... Day 7 system-design track ... Day 8 mock technical screen
criteria.reason: Sequencing matches the JD's recruiter→technical→onsite shape; next-round material (stories, past-work, coding/SQL) is front-loaded and onsite-only system design is correctly deferred to Day 7.

criteria.evidence: Priority queue table with columns Action / Skill to run / Maps to, e.g. 'Close the backend/API depth gap | build-curriculum (backend + SQL tracks) | technical screen + onsite coding'
criteria.reason: Every action names a concrete skill and the round/gap it serves; the gap→skill→round mapping is explicit.

criteria.evidence: 'Done' looks like: You can state a **real number** ... or you have a crisp, honest 'I'd want to confirm the exact figure...' with the dig already attempted. **No invented metrics, ever.**
criteria.reason: Concrete, honest readiness bar tied to this persona's actual risk (missing numbers), not platitudes; includes the anti-fabrication stance.

criteria.evidence: #1 Gather real numbers ... never invent ... ✅ ; 'System design fundamentals for the onsite (the only real learn-from-scratch track)'
criteria.reason: No mis-prioritization: the top must-do is collecting real metrics (the exact expectation for no-numbers), and fresh study is correctly limited to onsite system design rather than over-prepping any single round.
